speaker: Audience Member 2
text: Defeating authoritarian populism requires a counter-formula: promising national renewal through de-rigging the marketplace, optimism about an opportunity economy, and rebuilding social capital — rather than ceding the language of renewal and emergency to strongman politics.
context: Liam Byrne, UK Labour MP, citing his forthcoming book; described populist formula as 'appeasement abroad, autocracy at home, avarice everywhere.' [word uncertain in source]

addressed_in_artifacts.voice_name: Plato son of Ariston, of the deme Collytus — though when I write I withdraw my name from the page; you will hear me only through Socrates and the others.
